behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is not in local parsing logic; it is in model output quality. LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real neural translation judgments (grammar/meaning), which can vary across real model outputs. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed translator would always return curated good text and hide real quality regressions. REJECTION-GATE: Workflow was considered and rejected because the behavior only evaluates a single model output step, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service against the real translation model/API on a fixed multilingual evaluation set and assert semantic-adequacy/fluency thresholds (e.g., human-rated rubric or reference-based quality gates) rather than exact string match.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is a module handoff/orchestration failure (API client error -> fallback translator -> response shaping), so Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: real network/OS behavior is not required to reproduce this specific failure; an in-process stub returning 503-equivalent error is sufficient. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no dependence on model output quality, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-decision-governed stage handoff quality check. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered and rejected because behavior text only requires handling a 503 condition, not real timeout/socket/load semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + API client adapter + SQLite dictionary in-process. Stub the primary API adapter to return 503, verify caller receives a successful fallback translation and no surfaced error.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real failure boundary is between fallback logic and SQLite lookup/state, not a single pure function. REAL-INFRA: no external network/subprocess needed; local DB wiring is enough. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess beyond local DB file access, (b) no AI/ML output dependence, (c) no multi-agentic sequence, (d) no LLM-stage orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: Unit was considered and rejected because behavior explicitly requires validating retrieval from SQLite-backed stored phrases.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real local SQLite test DB seeded with known phrase pairs; call fallback translator through service/repository wiring and assert exact stored translation is returned.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior couples algorithm output with persistence side effect (compute + store), so single-function Unit would miss storage integration failures. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with in-process modules plus local DB; no real network/subprocess required. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required, (b) no dependence on model judgment quality for this behavior, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-orchestration dependency. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered and rejected because behavior text is about correct computation and persistence, not external infrastructure faults.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke quality tracker with deterministic primary/fallback strings, assert expected edit-distance value, then verify the metric row/field is persisted correctly in the local store.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration across modules (primary path selection + async quality logging), not just local logic. REAL-INFRA: real external HTTP/model call is not required for this behavior’s correctness; deterministic stubs for both translators can expose routing/async bugs. LLM-DEP: behavior does not evaluate translation quality, only control flow and side effects. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess, (b) no model-output-quality assertion, (c) no multiple agentic decisions sequence, (d) no planner/tool/critic-style LLM handoff correctness. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic was considered and rejected because behavior text does not require judging model output semantics, only selecting primary result and recording comparison asynchronously.
behaviors.test_description: Run service with stubbed primary and fallback translators both returning known strings; assert response uses primary output immediately and verify background task/event eventually writes comparison metric without blocking the response path.
